prompt_variant: You are a critical academic research verifier. Your task: determine if a bibliog...
_search_results:
**Yes, the paper exists.**

- **Exact title**: (Ir)rationality and Cognitive Biases in Large Language Models[1][2][4][5][8]
- **Authors**: Olivia Macmillan-Scott (University College London), Mirco Musolesi (University College London, University of Bologna)[1][2][4]
- **Venue**: Royal Society Open Science[4][6][8]
- **Year**: 2024[2][4][6][8]
- **DOI**: 10.1098/rsos.240255 (inferred from journal context and related citations)[4][6][8]

It originated as an arXiv preprint (arXiv:2402.09193, submitted February 14, 2024, revised February 15, 2024) with DOI 10.48550/arXiv.2402.09193[1][2], and was formally published in the journal, with an embargo until June 5, 2024[4]. The study evaluates seven LLMs on cognitive psychology tasks, finding they exhibit irrationality unlike human patterns, often with inconsistent responses[1][2][4][8].
